title: Sidebar: show ACL-granted private pages to authorized non-owners
Improve empty profile page text and populate example accounts
description: The text 'This person has not published a personal wiki landing page yet' is unhelpful. Replace with something minimal or hide entirely. Also populate jacobcole and other accounts with real content so the site doesn't look empty.
close_reason: Fixed empty profile text - only shows to owner now. Populated jacobcole profile.

title: Return 404 instead of 403 for unauthorized private pages to prevent enumeration
Cross-wiki wikilinks: [[/@user/wiki/page]] syntax
description: Platform-layer cross-wiki linking. Any wikilink starting with /@ resolves across wikis. No git submodules. Resolver checks target page existence across wiki_ids. Unresolved cross-wiki links render red-dashed. Documented in spec 2026-04-10.

title: Explore cards: hide empty stats instead of placeholder text
Split folder into standalone wiki
description: Promote a folder from personal wiki into its own project wiki. git subtree split extracts history, creates Wiki row, reindexes pages. Optionally replaces folder with cross-wiki link. One-way promotion only. v2 feature.

title: Profile sidebar: show all wikis for any user
Private band UI warning
description: No visual confirmation that <!-- private --> bands are working. Users need to see their page has private content. Show warning in rendered page view.
close_reason: Added private-band warning surfaces plus AST-aware band stripping.

title: v1.5 milestone
description: Near-term features building on v1 foundation: wikilink graph, structured data, API safety, viral growth.

title: D3 force-graph of wikilinks
description: Wikilink model already tracks [[link]] refs between pages. Need: (1) API endpoint to dump graph as JSON (nodes + edges), (2) page with D3 force layout visualization. ~80 lines of D3. High demo value.

title: JSON-LD structured data on pages
description: Add <script type=application/ld+json> block to page template with @type: Article, title, author, dateModified. Trivial effort, free SEO/discoverability.

title: Optimistic locking (If-Match / ETag / 409)
description: Pages have git commit SHAs. Return SHA as ETag on GET, require If-Match on PUT, 409 if stale. Prevents silent data loss from concurrent edits.

title: Twitter auth / tweet-to-verify signup
description: Signup requires tweeting a specific post and pasting tweet URL to verify. Viral distribution + lightweight sybil resistance. Agent-compatible via Twitter API key.

title: Merge People into Explore (single page, people + wikis)
Per-wiki llms.txt endpoint
description: /@user/wiki/llms.txt — site-wide exists but per-wiki version is what agents want when working with a specific wiki. Trivial to add.

title: Consistent nav across all pages (landing + inner)
Design community index wiki (@wikihub as curated meta-index)
description:
The @wikihub/wikihub wiki should serve as a curated community index — an 'awesome list of awesome lists.' User stories:

1. A user creates a great 'Best Body Masters List' wiki. They want it featured.
2. The community wiki has a page like 'Best Fitness Wikis' that links to it with a blurb.
3. A visitor lands on @wikihub and can browse curated categories pointing to the best user wikis.

This is different from /explore (algorithmic, by stars/activity). The community wiki is editorial — someone decides what's featured.

Key questions to resolve:
- Is @wikihub/wikihub the right place for this, or should there be a separate @wikihub/featured wiki?
- How do categories work? Pages in the wiki like 'Best AI Tools Wikis', 'Best Reading Lists'?
- Should featured wikis get a badge/indicator on their own pages?
- Who can edit the community index? Just admins, or suggested-edit PRs from users?
- How does this interplay with the Explore page? Does Explore pull from the community wiki's editorial picks?

For v1: probably just manually curated pages in @wikihub/wikihub that link to user wikis with [[cross-wiki wikilinks]] or plain URLs. The index IS the wiki — no special machinery needed. Editorial curation is just writing markdown.

Reference: ListHub had similar brainstorming about community lists as indexes of other lists.

title: Sort accounts and wikis by recent activity on Explore page
Wikilink rewriting on page rename
description: Rename works but doesn't scan/rewrite [[old-path]] wikilinks. Silent link breakage = data integrity issue. Spec requires atomic rewrite of all referencing pages.
close_reason: Implemented rename/move wikilink rewriting and atomic repo updates in PATCH page handling.

title: Add people browsing and folder creation UX
description: Design and implement a way to browse users on the system, improve person/profile discoverability relative to wiki browsing, review sidebar universality, and add a simple UI affordance for creating folders. Update the spec and ship the first pass in product.
acceptance_criteria: Explore or adjacent navigation exposes people browsing; user profile clearly distinguishes personal wiki and other wikis; folder creation flow exists in web UI; spec is updated; e2e coverage added.
notes: Implement people browsing on Explore, strengthen profile as person page, add first-pass folder creation UI, update spec, and add e2e coverage.
close_reason: Implemented people browsing on /explore and /people, strengthened /@username as the canonical person page with personal wiki first, added folder creation UI via index.md scaffolding, updated the spec, and added e2e coverage.

title: v3 milestone
description: Advanced features: realtime collab, org/multi-tenant, encryption, moderation tooling.

title: Paste-unstructured-text → LLM generates wiki
description: Server-hosted coding agent with platform key. Leaning headless Claude Code over raw Anthropic tool-use. Needs agent orchestration layer.

title: Link-share token expiry
description: v1 link tokens are permanent. Add expiry syntax or Postgres-side override.

title: D3 force-graph render of wikilinks (site-wide)
description: Cross-wiki graph visualization. Extends the per-wiki v1.5 graph to full platform view.

title: git-crypt escape hatch for encrypted wikis
description: Encrypted personal wikis pattern from jacobcole.net/labs.

title: Multi-human-one-agent session semantics
description: Multiple humans collaborating through a single agent session.

title: Wikipedia-grade moderation tooling
description: Edit filters, trust levels, auto-revert bots. Full moderation suite.

title: Org / multi-tenant surface
description: Anthropic intranet target. Principal table shape is ready. Multi-tenancy / org support.

title: Signed-in mode, link-share mode ACL
description: Deferred ACL modes: signed-in, link-share, link:token:role grants, group:name:role grants.

title: URL / repo connect (import from public git repos)
description: git clone --bare + walk tree + index into Postgres. Plumbing exists in git_sync.py. Need UI form and background worker.

title: HTML / Google Sites importer
description: Import existing HTML sites/Google Sites into wiki format.

title: Inline diff UI for suggested edits
description: Fork is v1; suggest-edits-back-upstream PR-like mechanic (diff format, list UI, accept/reject, cherry-pick). No current user demand yet.

title: Friends-list / group creation UI
description: Grant syntax is v1, the management UI is v2+. Group CRUD, member management, use in ACL grants.

title: Real-time collaborative editing
description: CRDT/OT. Massive undertaking. Async-first, not biased toward realtime.

title: Server-hosted cloud agent
description: Talk-to-an-agent-we-host via web UI, per-user folder sandbox via UNIX permissions.

title: Featured curation admin surface
description: v1 uses most-starred automatically. Admin override for manual curation.

title: SFTP upload path
description: Session-batched commits, SSH key endpoint, sshfs/rsync compatible. Needs SSH server daemon.

title: Import curated ListHub content to WikiHub
description: Import select ListHub lists as wikis on WikiHub. Good candidates: Body Masters, Autodidacts, Mental State Hacks, Startup Ideas, Bay Area Activities, Supplements. Put on @jacobcole account. Feature best ones in @wikihub community index.
close_reason: Imported 7 ListHub items to @jacobcole/curated-lists. Created Featured Wikis page in @wikihub community wiki.

title: Review and triage spec/code audit findings (2026-04-10)
description: Full spec/code audit conducted 2026-04-10 by parallel Claude + Codex review. Found 44 issues across 4 severity tiers. Spec contradictions (category 1) already fixed in-session. Remaining: 4 critical code bugs, 10 high-priority gaps, ~20 medium missing features, ~10 low nits. Full findings in docs/audit-2026-04-10.md. This ticket: review the audit, create individual tickets for items worth tracking separately, and prioritize the critical 4 for immediate fix.
notes: Implementing the 2026-04-10 audit list end-to-end, starting with critical items and verifying with tests after each logical batch.
close_reason: Implemented the 2026-04-10 audit list and updated the checklist in docs/audit-2026-04-10.md.

title: Polish people cards with visibility badges and richer profile metadata
description: Follow up on the first-pass people browsing feature by adding spec-consistent visibility badges on explore/profile cards, richer personal-wiki metadata on person cards, and finalizing mobile polish for the person-page sidebar.
acceptance_criteria: People/explore/profile cards show visibility affordances where relevant; person cards surface better profile metadata from personal wiki frontmatter/body; mobile person page remains coherent with sidebar actions.

title: Add one-click magic-link sign-in for agents
description: Let an authenticated agent generate a short-lived one-click sign-in link for the account owner, so the browser session can be established without typing credentials or pasting API keys. Update the spec and add e2e coverage.
acceptance_criteria: API endpoint returns a one-click login URL for the authenticated account; visiting the link logs the user into a browser session and invalidates the token; link expires; spec updated; e2e coverage added.
notes: Implement short-lived single-use magic login links for agent-generated browser sign-in, update spec, and add e2e coverage.
close_reason: Implemented short-lived single-use magic sign-in links via API and browser route, documented the agent flow, updated the spec, and added e2e coverage.

title: v2 milestone
description: Core platform features: abuse protection, social features, content import, polish.

title: IP rate limit on writes (10/min/IP)
description: Anti-abuse priority #1. flask-limiter already referenced in app/__init__.py. Decorator config on write endpoints. Infra-level, not moderation.

title: Comments on pages and wikis
description: ACL vocabulary already reserves 'comment' role. Need Comment model, API endpoints, UI component. Social feature that drives engagement.

title: Omni-convert upload (PDF/DOCX → markdown)
description: Pandoc handles DOCX→MD well. PDF is messier (pdftotext or LLM). Could ship DOCX-only first as quick win — zip upload infra already exists.

title: Landing page live background
description: Featured/popular wiki cards slowly drifting/orbiting behind hero section. Pure CSS animation. Needs enough public wikis to look good — ship after dogfood migrations populate platform.

title: Panic button: disable anonymous writes per wiki
description: Anti-abuse priority #2. Toggle to instantly disable anonymous writes on a wiki. Quick emergency response for spam/abuse.

title: Revert last N anonymous edits button
description: Anti-abuse priority #3. Web UI button to bulk-revert recent anonymous edits. Recovery path after spam attacks.

title: MCP server endpoint (/mcp)
description: Discovery endpoints exist (.well-known/mcp/server-card.json, /.well-known/mcp) but the actual /mcp endpoint serving MCP protocol is missing. Tools: whoami, search, read_page, list_pages, create_page, update_page, append_section, delete_page, set_visibility, share, create_wiki, fork_wiki, commit_log. This is the whole thesis — wikihub is GitHub for LLM wikis.
close_reason: Implemented /mcp with tool listing/call proxy behavior and wired discovery manifests.

title: Populate @wikihub official wiki with starter content
close_reason: Created 5 pages: index, Getting Started, Agent Integration, API Reference, Examples
